content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission


November 25, 1991


Valerie S. Creel, Chairwoman
Rio Dell Recall Committee
P. O. Box 365
Rio Dell, CA  95562

Re: Your Request for Informal
Assistance
Our File No. I-91-487

Dear Ms. Creel:

You have requested advice concerning the campaign reporting
obligations of the Rio Dell Recall Committee (the "Committee")
under the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 Since your request
is general in nature, we are treating it as a request for informal
assistance.2

QUESTION

What are the campaign disclosure obligations of the Rio Dell
Recall Committee?

CONCLUSION

The committee has no campaign disclosure obligations until it
qualifies as a "Committee" under the Act.  The committee's
potential disclosure obligations are discussed in the Analysis,
below.

FACTS

You have indicated that the Rio Dell Recall Committee was

1  Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq.  All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2  Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sections
18329(c)(3).)


428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916)322-5660

Page 2
Creel, Valerie



formed to recall three members of the Rio Dell City Council. The
committee has not yet solicited contributions but has received an
anonymous contribution of $200.

ANALYSIS

Section 82013(a) defines "committee" as:

...any person or combination of persons who directly or
indirectly:

(a) Receives contributions totaling one thousand
dollars ($1,000) or more in a calendar year....

Therefore, the committee will not incur campaign disclosure
obligations until it has received contributions totaling $1,000 or
more in a calendar year. To determine if $1,000 has been
received, you must count all monetary and non-monetary
contributions received, including loans received and any personal
funds spent by you or other committee members in connection with
qualification or passage of the measure.

Section 84101(a) requires every committee qualified under
Section 82013(a) to file a statement of organization (Form 410)
with the Secretary of State within 10 days after receiving
contributions totaling $1,000 or more in a calendar year. In
addition to filing the original and one copy of Form 410 with the
Secretary of State, the committee must file a copy with the local
filing officer with whom it will file the originals of its
campaign reports, which in your case will be the City Clerk of Rio
Dell. (Section 84215.)

If the committee qualifies under Section 82013(a), it will
also be required to file periodic campaign reports (Form 420 or
450). If it becomes a "committee" during the period in which you
are attempting to qualify the measure, the committee's first
report will be due 21 days after any petitions are filed, or 21
days after the deadline for filing petitions, whichever is
earlier. The closing date for the period covered by this report
will be seven days prior to the deadline for filing the report.
(Section 84200.5(f).) The committee also will be required to file
semi-annual campaign statements each year and, if the recall
measure qualifies for the ballot, pre-election statements must be
filed prior to the election.

Semi-annual statements are due no later than July 31 for the
period ending June 30, and no later than January 31 for the period
ending December 31. (Section 84200.) The deadlines for pre-
election campaign statements are determined by the date of the
election. (Section 84200.8.) Campaign statements must be filed
until the committee is eligible to terminate. (Sections 82013,
84214.) The City Clerk of Rio Dell can provide you with the
appropriate forms, filing dates, and the Commission's campaign
information manual (Manual D--Campaign Manual for Committees

Page 3
Creel, Valerie


Primarily Formed to Support or Oppose the Qualification, Passage,
or Defeat of a Ballot Measure).

You should also be aware that Section 84304 provides:

No person shall make an anonymous contribution or
contributions to a candidate, committee or any other person
totaling one hundred dollars ($100) or more in calendar year.
An anonymous contribution of one hundred dollars ($100) or
more shall not be kept by the intended recipient but instead
shall be promptly paid to the Secretary of State for deposit
in the General Fund of the state.

If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916)
322-5662.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Wayne P. Imberi
Political Reform Consultant

sections.analysis:
Section 82013(a) defines "committee" as:

...any person or combination of persons who directly or indirectly:

(a) Receives contributions totaling one thousand dollars ($1,000) or more in a calendar year....

Therefore, the committee will not incur campaign disclosure obligations until it has received contributions totaling $1,000 or more in a calendar year. To determine if $1,000 has been received, you must count all monetary and non-monetary contributions received, including loans received and any personal funds spent by you or other committee members in connection with qualification or passage of the measure.

Section 84101(a) requires every committee qualified under Section 82013(a) to file a statement of organization (Form 410) with the Secretary of State within 10 days after receiving contributions totaling $1,000 or more in a calendar year. In addition to filing the original and one copy of Form 410 with the Secretary of State, the committee must file a copy with the local filing officer with whom it will file the originals of its campaign reports, which in your case will be the City Clerk of Rio Dell. (Section 84215.)

If the committee qualifies under Section 82013(a), it will also be required to file periodic campaign reports (Form 420 or 450). If it becomes a "committee" during the period in which you are attempting to qualify the measure, the committee's first report will be due 21 days after any petitions are filed, or 21 days after the deadline for filing petitions, whichever is earlier. The closing date for the period covered by this report will be seven days prior to the deadline for filing the report. (Section 84200.5(f).) The committee also will be required to file semi-annual campaign statements each year and, if the recall measure qualifies for the ballot, pre-election statements must be filed prior to the election.

Semi-annual statements are due no later than July 31 for the period ending June 30, and no later than January 31 for the period ending December 31. (Section 84200.) The deadlines for pre-election campaign statements are determined by the date of the election. (Section 84200.8.) Campaign statements must be filed until the committee is eligible to terminate. (Sections 82013, 84214.) The City Clerk of Rio Dell can provide you with the appropriate forms, filing dates, and the Commission's campaign information manual (Manual D--Campaign Manual for Committees

Primarily Formed to Support or Oppose the Qualification, Passage, or Defeat of a Ballot Measure).

You should also be aware that Section 84304 provides:

No person shall make an anonymous contribution or contributions to a candidate, committee or any other person totaling one hundred dollars ($100) or more in calendar year. An anonymous contribution of one hundred dollars ($100) or more shall not be kept by the intended recipient but instead shall be promptly paid to the Secretary of State for deposit in the General Fund of the state.
